Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: ATP-binding cassette subfamily G / pleiotropic drug resistance transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM fungal signaling; parasitic plant germination cue; plant developmental hormone when retained internally
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly in sub-epidermal/hypodermal passage cells of lateral roots; induced by phosphate starvation, AM colonization, GR24, and NAA; also reported in vascular/nodal shoot tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; AM fungal colonization; GR24; NAA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone export into the rhizosphere without strongly altering root extract levels; mutation severely reduces exudation and AM symbiosis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; transporter screen; localization; root extract versus exudate strigolactone measurements
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPDR1 is the first identified strigolactone exporter. 2. Phpdr1 mutants retain near-normal root strigolactone content but show strongly reduced exudation. 3. Reduced exudation decreases AM fungal interaction, demonstrating active export controls rhizosphere signaling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent target to reduce parasitic weed stimulation while potentially preserving endogenous architecture-related strigolactone functions better than biosynthetic knockouts.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport role within vascular/xylem loading or unloading remains unresolved; branching phenotype is mild despite nodal expression.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced mycorrhizal symbiosis due to lower rhizosphere strigolactone signals.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago truncatula
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago truncatula
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed carotenoid substrate for downstream strigolactone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling, AM fungal hyphal branching, parasitic seed germination, inhibition of shoot branching/tillering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in roots under phosphate deficiency; expressed in stem vascular bundle in rice
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root/stem vascular-associated tissues; phosphate starvation responsive
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; nitrogen starvation context via NSP1/NSP2 in Medicago
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for strigolactone formation; loss reduces strigolactone production and causes high-tillering/branching phenotypes rescued by GR24
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; in vitro enzyme assay with synthetic carotenoid isomers; GR24 rescue
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry step establishing the required 9-cis substrate
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D27 acts early in strigolactone biosynthesis upstream of MAX1. 2. D27 was shown to possess all-trans- to 9-cis-β-carotene isomerase activity. 3. D27 is required for endogenous strigolactone production and related developmental responses.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for tuning total strigolactone production to modify tillering/branching and rhizosphere signaling to AM fungi or parasitic weeds.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper summarizes that Arabidopsis D27 ortholog also acts upstream of MAX1; in Medicago D27 transcription depends on NSP1/NSP2.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Physcomitrella patens
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Physcomitrella patens
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stereo-specifically cleaves 9-cis-β-carotene to yield 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal and β-ionone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct precursor for CCD8-mediated carlactone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling, AM fungal hyphal branching, parasitic seed germination, inhibition of shoot branching/tillering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rice HTD1 expressed throughout vascular parenchyma cells of the root and in stem vascular bundle
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Vascular-associated expression in root/stem; summarized across species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for strigolactone biosynthesis; deficiency lowers strigolactone levels and alters branching/tillering and rhizosphere signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; heterologous expression; in vitro substrate-specific enzyme assay with synthetic carotenoid isomers
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early cleavage step in core pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD7 is a core biosynthetic enzyme in strigolactone formation. 2. It specifically uses 9-cis-β-carotene rather than all-trans substrate. 3. Its product is the immediate substrate for CCD8.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulation can reduce strigolactone output to control parasitic weed germination and plant architecture, but may affect beneficial mycorrhization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review notes PpCCD8 knockout reduces some but not all putative strigolactones in moss, implying pathway divergence downstream or partial redundancy; this nuance concerns pathway conservation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Pisum sativum; Physcomitrella patens
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Pisum sativum; Physcomitrella patens
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts 9-cis-β-apo-10'-carotenal into carlactone through unusual multi-step chemistry including isomerization and oxidative rearrangements
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling, AM fungal hyphal branching, parasitic seed germination, inhibition of shoot branching/tillering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rice D10 expressed throughout vascular parenchyma cells of the root and in stem vascular bundle; Arabidopsis MAX4 highly expressed in columella root cap and induced in (pro)vasculature and cortex by NAA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root vascular/parenchyma and root cap-associated expression; nodal expression near buds in Arabidopsis summarized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for strigolactone formation; mutants are strigolactone deficient with elevated tillering/branching and altered root phenotypes, rescued by GR24/carlactone-related activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; in vitro enzyme assay; metabolite identification of carlactone; GR24 rescue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CCD8 converts the CCD7 product into carlactone, a strigolactone-like intermediate. 2. This discovery greatly simplified the previously proposed pathway. 3. CCD8 is essential for endogenous strigolactone-dependent developmental and rhizosphere functions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major engineering node for altering total strigolactone output, tillering, and parasite stimulation with likely trade-offs in AM symbiosis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In Physcomitrella, CCD8 knockout reduced only some detected strigolactones, suggesting noncanonical downstream biosynthesis or annotation uncertainty.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Medicago truncatula
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Oryza sativa; Medicago truncatula
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid-derived strigolactone pathway downstream of carlactone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely precursor toward 5-deoxystrigol; exact product unresolved in this review
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Required cytochrome P450 step downstream of D27/CCD7/CCD8, proposed to catalyze oxidative conversion(s) from carlactone toward 5-deoxystrigol
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for downstream conversion to canonical strigolactones
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signaling, AM fungal hyphal branching, parasitic seed germination, inhibition of shoot branching/tillering
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis MAX1 predominantly expressed in root vascular tissue; in stem cambial/xylem-associated parenchyma; Medicago root expression depends on NSP1/NSP2 under N starvation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root vascular tissue; stem vascular cambial region; nodal tissue near buds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; nitrogen starvation in Medicago
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for strigolactone biosynthesis and normal shoot branching control
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mutant evidence; strigolactone measurements; expression localization
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Downstream essential step, exact control-point status not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAX1 is an essential CYP required for strigolactone biosynthesis. 2. It acts downstream of D27, CCD7, and CCD8 and is proposed to convert carlactone toward 5-deoxystrigol. 3. Multiple MAX1-like genes in rice suggest possible redundancy/diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for modifying specific strigolactone structures rather than abolishing the whole pathway, potentially allowing tuning of biological specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact catalytic activity was unresolved at the time of this review; rice contains multiple MAX1 homologs and moss lacks an obvious MAX1 ortholog, indicating pathway divergence.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for root expression of D27 and a MAX1 ortholog under nutrient stress
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM symbiosis signaling and plant developmental regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strigolactone biosynthesis; nsp1 mutants fail to induce D27 and do not produce normal strigolactone levels
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Nutrient-responsive positive regulator of core biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root regulatory context under nitrogen/phosphate starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; gene expression analysis; promoter cis-element association
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP1 is required for nutrient-responsive expression of D27 and a MAX1 ortholog in Medicago roots. 2. It likely directly regulates strigolactone biosynthetic genes via nodulation-responsive promoter elements. 3. NSP1 links nutrient signaling and strigolactone production.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to coordinate symbiosis-related strigolactone production under nutrient stress rather than constitutively altering pathway enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper presents NSP1 as one of the first transcriptional regulators implicated in strigolactone biosynthesis rather than as a central universal strigolactone regulator across plants.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering NSP1 may affect nodulation-related processes beyond strigolactones.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Contributes to nutrient-responsive induction of D27 and MAX1 ortholog; weaker D27 induction persists in nsp2 than in nsp1
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: AM symbiosis signaling and plant developmental regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes normal strigolactone production/composition; nsp2 mutants alter strigolactone profile from didehydro-orobanchol to orobanchol
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent regulator affecting both amount and composition of strigolactones
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root regulatory context under nutrient starvation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; gene expression analysis; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NSP2 contributes to induction of D27 and MAX1 ortholog expression in Medicago roots. 2. It affects strigolactone composition, shifting didehydro-orobanchol/orobanchol balance. 3. NSP2 links nutrient signaling to strigolactone biosynthesis and diversification.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning strigolactone composition to favor AM interactions while reducing parasitic weed stimulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review highlights metabolite composition changes in nsp2 as a tool to discover downstream tailoring enzymes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects on nodulation/symbiosis pathways.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Strigolactone perception/signaling controlling developmental responses to strigolactones
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Unknown downstream protein substrates in SCFMAX2 pathway; BRC1/TB1/FC1 expression responds downstream
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required for strigolactone responses and associated downstream transcriptional outputs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Strigolactone perception via D14/DAD2; also required for karrikin responses
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal control of branching, germination, and root architecture
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential for strigolactone responsiveness including branching inhibition and germination responses; mutants are insensitive to GR24
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central signal transduction component required for conversion of strigolactone presence into physiological response
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis e-FP browser shows relatively high expression in root elongation zone; high near nodal tissues close to buds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root elongation zone; nodal tissue near buds
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GR24; karrikins; adverse germination temperature/light signaling context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; hormone application; protein complex evidence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MAX2 is a core downstream signaling component required for plant responses to strigolactones. 2. It functions in an SCF ubiquitin ligase complex analogous to auxin signaling. 3. MAX2 is also involved in karrikin-related signaling and seed germination/light-related responses.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Powerful node for changing strigolactone sensitivity, but likely highly pleiotropic due to broad signaling functions.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a biosynthetic gene; review treats it as core perception/signaling machinery. Specific receptor identity was unresolved, but MAX2 is indispensable for response.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong pleiotropy affecting photomorphogenesis, germination, branching and karrikin signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: SCFMAX2 complex with SKP1 and CUL1-type components

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida; Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Oryza sativa; Petunia hybrida; Arabidopsis thaliana
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ligand binding/hydrolysis-dependent activation of signaling and protein interaction with MAX2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enables downstream strigolactone-responsive developmental gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hormonal control of branching, germination, root architecture, other development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for strigolactone responses; binds and hydrolyzes GR24/strigolactone analogs and promotes interaction with MAX2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary candidate receptor determining ligand specificity and signal initiation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis relatively high in root elongation zone according to e-FP browser
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; ligand binding assay; hydrolysis assay; crystal structure; protein-protein interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. D14/DAD2 is essential for strigolactone responses in monocots and dicots. 2. Petunia DAD2 binds and hydrolyzes GR24, and its catalytic activity is required for interaction with PhMAX2. 3. Structural data support its role as a receptor-like hydrolase with ligand specificity implications.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for altering strigolactone sensitivity/specificity without necessarily changing biosynthetic levels.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review discusses D14 as receptor candidate rather than definitively established receptor; D14-like proteins may mediate distinct ligand responses including karrikins.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad developmental effects due to altered hormone perception.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with MAX2 after ligand hydrolysis

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Arabidopsis thaliana; Pisum sativum; Oryza sativa
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Bud outgrowth response downstream of strigolactone signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional control of bud dormancy/growth state
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts downstream of strigolactones; expression maintained/increased by strigolactones and antagonized by cytokinin
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Buds and associated tissues affected by transported/local strigolactones
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mediates inhibition of bud outgrowth/tillering downstream of strigolactones
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major downstream transcriptional switch integrating strigolactone and cytokinin signals for branching control
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in and below dormant axillary buds
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Dormant axillary buds and subtending tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotypes; hormone application studies; expression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BRC1/TB1/FC1 functions downstream of strigolactones in suppressing bud outgrowth. 2. Strigolactones antagonize cytokinin effects at the level of BRC1/TB1 expression. 3. Mutants display increased branching/tillering, confirming decisive downstream control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for architecture breeding where downstream response modulation is preferred over changing total strigolactone biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene regulates developmental response rather than strigolactone biosynthesis itself, but is repeatedly highlighted in the review as a primary summarized core signaling output.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Architecture changes may affect yield components and source-sink balance.
